extraction.one_line_summary: PHILLIPS Jasper won the longest stage of the Vuelta (143.5 miles) in a shattered uphill sprint after headwinds and rain fractured the peloton.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma escorted peloton despite Roglic in no jersey danger, suggesting control tactics prioritized race rhythm over energy conservation.
13-rider breakaway caught despite 143-mile distance, revealing headwind nullified traditional escape mathematics on ultra-long stages.
Sunweb placed two riders in breakaway after yesterday's success, showing teams recalibrating break investment after Lastra stage win.
Final sprint peloton shattered in final 5km despite flat run-in, indicating accumulated fatigue trumps sprinter team organization on longest stages.

rider_signals.MARTIN Guillermo: positive: accumulated near-unassailable KOM lead (85 points, 51 ahead) by targeting early categorized climbs before peloton engagement
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: team burned energy escorting peloton despite massive GC/points leads, possible indication of controlling race tempo for future mountain stages
rider_signals.MORKOV Michael: positive: led out Phillips despite UAE typically lacking sprint train depth, showing veteran positioning compensates for team strength
rider_signals.ACKERMAN Pascal: negative: contested sprint finish decision despite not winning, suggesting positioning desperation rather than genuine sprint superiority
